GCC Market Expansion

**Kotak Inst Eqt on Hindalco: Maintains SELL, TP ₹800**  

**US Scrap Spreads Expanding**  
- US scrap spreads widening; scrap at **~47% of Midwest P1020** vs **70% last year** and **58% CY2025 average**.  
- **Higher LME aluminium prices** from Middle East conflict and **lower scrap demand** (led by Novelis) driving spreads.  

**Novelis Challenges Persist**  
- Peers gaining, but Novelis sees **limited benefits** due to plant outages and can sheet diversion to auto.  
- **Margins bottomed in 3QFY26**; production normalization only from **2HFY27E**.  

**India Operations Strong**  
- **India business well-positioned** for commodity price tailwinds.  

**Key Takeaway:** Kotak cites rich valuations and Novelis recovery delays despite India positives, maintaining SELL on Hindalco.

Sharp volatility in crude and physical commodity shortages can cause short term upheavals in earnings
Can be difficult to take directional views - particularly in size

*GS on L&T*
Buy, TP Rs 4420, earlier Rs4,950
Stock is down 18% since March 2nd (vs BSE Sensex -7%) driven by concerns around
(1) Execution of the current projects in the Middle East
(2) Possibility of reduced capex in the region impacting L&T’s prospect base in FY27 driving lower revenue growth in outer years
While near term concerns on execution may be valid, see medium term prospect base unlikely to change even though deferral for a few quarters is likely given ongoing conflict
lower revenue for Q4 assuming slower execution over the last 2 weeks of March 2025
Carry forward weak execution into Q1FY27
Also lower core order inflow for FY27E from 7.7% earlier to 1.1% now assuming almost no ordering in international business for 1M.

*GS on BEL*
Buy TP Rs 470
CO  secured an additional order of Rs  10.1bn for Communication equipment, Radar Warning Receiver (RWR), fire control system, electro optic sight, fire detection & warning system for fighter aircraft, high energy laser, automatic train supervision system, headup display, and software solutions
Total order inflow for FY26 (thus far) is Rs 216.2bn
Orders received so far in FY26 exclude first tranche of NGC order (INR 30-40bn), Shatrughat (INR 30bn) and LRU for LCAs (INR 24bn) which expect to come in Q4FY26
Thus, expect management’s guidance of INR270bn to be met.

Sell, TP Rs 1260

Management remains focused on driving better-than-industry growth and 15% EBIT margin in FY27E, in line with the earlier guidance.
Impact of current macro situation as well as AI-related trends remain key watch, in near to medium term
TechM has executed well in recent quarters: Valuations currently are at 18x FY27E on consensus EPS estimates, a premium to almost all the large cap peers (TCS – 16x, Infosys – 16x, HCLT – 18x, Wipro – 14x); better-than-industry growth in FY27E will be key for continued outperformance.

Vertical-wise demand commentary remains intact with BFSI continuing to see tailwinds for all four companies, tech is doing well for HCL & TCS & retail, auto and healthcare verticals remain soft
A few cos flagged a slight delay in decision making by clients due to launch of the latest AI tools to explore their true potential and due to the ensuing Middle East (ME) crises
On ME crises, direct revenue exposure for all four companies is in low single digits but broader macro impact will depend on the duration of the crises and impact on inflation, interest rates, GDP growth and broader IT services spending
Deal pipelines remain strong and valuations for India’s IT sector at its 10-year average now look highly attractive

O-PF on INFY, TechM, TCS and LTIM

reports.text: CLEAN MAX ENVIRO: GUIDANCE: OVER 1.5 GW RE POWER SALES CAPACITY ADDITION IN FY 2026-2027 - INVESTOR PRESENTATION

Indian Defence: 7 Interconnected Value Chains  

Defence Ecosystem Pyramid  
India's defence isn't one sector—it's a pyramid of value chains where visibility ≠ profitability and order books ≠ value capture.  
Deeper layers (materials, electronics) hold higher moats despite lower attention.  

1. Platform Builders (Visible Top Layer)  
- Build final systems: Aircraft, warships, submarines, missiles, artillery.  
- Key listed: HAL, Bharat Dynamics, Mazagon Dock, Cochin Shipyard, GRSE.  

2. Electronics, Radar & Avionics (High-Margin Brain)  
- Radar, EW, sensors, avionics, guidance—market to $11.35 bn by 2032.  
- Key listed: BEL, Data Patterns, Astra Microwave, Paras Defence, Zen Tech.  

3. Weapons, Missiles & Ammo (Consumption Play)  
- Missiles, drones, artillery—shifts from capex to recurring demand.  
- Key listed: Bharat Dynamics, Bharat Forge, Zen Technologies.  

4. Precision Engineering & Forgings (Industrial Backbone)  
- Forgings, structures, engines—enables manufacturing depth.  
- Key listed: Bharat Forge, L&T.  

5. Materials & Alloys (Undervalued Bottleneck)  
- Specialty metals, composites, explosives—true indigenisation constraint.  
- Proxy listed: Mishra Dhatu Nigam, Solar Industries, Hindustan Zinc.  

6. MRO & Lifecycle (Annuity Revenue)  
- Maintenance, upgrades, spares for long-term cash flows.  
- Key exposure: HAL, BEL.  

7. Emerging Warfare (Drones, Space, AI)  
- Drones, anti-drone, ISR, space defence—fastest growth.  
- Key listed: Paras Defence, Zen Tech, Data Patterns.  

Key Takeaway: True defence winners build irreplaceable capabilities in mid-to-bottom layers, not just headline contracts.

**JPMorgan on Oil & Gas: Volatility Persists Amid Geopolitical Risks**  

**Short-Term Earnings Upheaval**  
- **Crude volatility and commodity shortages** causing sharp earnings swings.  
- **Directional bets challenging**, especially at scale, given uncertainty.  

**Oil Price & Stock Outlook**  
- **FY27 oil prices likely higher** than mid-February forecasts across most scenarios.  
- **OMC stocks poised to rebound** on ceasefire announcements.  
- **Higher oil to boost ONGC, GAIL, Reliance** via positive medium-term revisions.  

**Relative Performance**  
- **GAIL underperformed NIFTY** since war began; **ONGC and Reliance outperformed** market.  

**Key Takeaway:** JPMorgan sees elevated oil prices supporting upstream majors despite near-term volatility.

**Jefferies on Chemicals: ME Dependency Disrupts Supply Chains**  

**Sector Impact Cascade**  
- **India's heavy reliance on Middle East feedstocks** causing widespread supply chain disruptions.  
- **Fertilizers hit hardest**, followed by paints, adhesives, and related segments.  
- **Crop protection firms** face risks from delayed kharif sowing timelines.  

**Margin & Inventory Outlook**

- **Q4 sees inventory gains**, but **operating rate cuts and margin pressure** expected from April onwards.  

**Key Takeaway:** Jefferies flags near-term supply disruptions and margin risks for Indian chemicals, with Q4 inventory gains offering temporary relief.

@Stockupdate9
Sector which is "dead" ⏬

India’s power sector is entering one of its biggest structural upcycles in decades.  

Current Capacity Snapshot  
- Installed capacity at ~525 GW: Thermal ~240 GW (Coal ~220, Gas ~20), Renewables ~250+ GW, Nuclear ~7 GW.  
- Key generation players: NTPC, Adani Power, Tata Power, JSW Energy.  

Upcoming Additions (FY26–FY31)  
- Plans to add ~50 GW renewables annually (~250 GW over 5 years) and ~40–50 GW thermal.  
- Total capacity could reach ~750–800 GW by 2030.  

Structural Demand Gap  
- Electricity ~18% of India’s total energy vs ~30% in China and ~20–22% global average.  
- Under-electrification offers long growth runway.  

Transmission Bottleneck  
- Generation scaling faster than evacuation: Solar setup ~12 months vs transmission ~30–36 months.  
- Needs ~₹2.5–3 lakh crore transmission capex by 2030.  

Key Beneficiary Categories  
- Transformers: CG Power, Hitachi Energy India, Siemens Energy India, GE T&D India, TRIL, Voltamp Transformers.  
- Cables & Wires: Polycab, KEI Industries, Havells, Finolex Cables, RR Kabel.  
- Conductors: Apar Industries, Sterlite Power, Diamond Power Infrastructure.  
- EPC/T&D: Kalpataru Projects, KEC International, L&T Power T&D, Techno Electric, Skipper.  
- Towers/Structures: Skipper, Kalpataru Projects.  
- Grid Owners: Power Grid Corp, Adani Energy Solutions.  

Cost Economics  
- Capex per GW: Solar ~₹4,500–5,000 Cr, Thermal ~₹14,000–16,000 Cr, Nuclear ~₹20,000–25,000 Cr.  
- Solar tariffs ~₹2–2.5/unit, among lowest globally.  

Demand Accelerators  
- Manufacturing (PLI), EVs, data centers, cooling, green hydrogen.  
- Power demand growth to shift from ~0.7–0.8x GDP to ~1–1.2x GDP.  

Investment Lens  
- Generation grabs headlines, transmission gets sustained capex, equipment enjoys leverage.  
- Full chain participation: Generation → Transmission → Transformers → Conductors → Cables → EPC → Connectivity.  

Bottom Line  
India’s power upcycle spans generation, transmission, distribution, and storage – fueled by cheap renewables and rising demand.

🗞 BEAT THE STREET BULLETIN 🗞
Date: 17 March 2026
Telegram: https://t.me/BeatTheStreetnews

📍 ORDER WIN

KALPATARU PROJECTS INTERNATIONAL: Bags ₹2,471 crore orders across metro, T&D and B&F segments in India & overseas.

PURAVANKARA: Subsidiary Starworth Infra wins ₹280.35 crore construction order from VIT Vellore.

CEIGALL INDIA: Wins ₹207 crore urban infra projects from Punjab Infrastructure Development Board.

TIME TECHNOPLAST: Secures ₹115.56 crore order for CNG cascades from PSU.

PROSTARM INFO SYSTEMS: Declared L1 bidder for ₹90.44 crore UPS systems order from WBMSC.

CHOICE INTERNATIONAL: Subsidiaries bag ~₹55 crore multi-state government mandates.

MAGELLANIC CLOUD: Subsidiary receives ₹1.55 crore CCTV project LOI from RailTel.

---

📍 ACQUISITION

TECH MAHINDRA: Acquires remaining 20% stake in Tech Mahindra Arabia for ₹206 crore, making it a wholly owned subsidiary.

MITCON: To acquire up to 49% stake in two solar companies to expand renewable portfolio.

CLEAN MAX ENVIRO: Acquires 100% stake in Kintech Solarbikaner for ₹38.06 crore.

SAMVARDHANA MOTHERSON: Completes 100% acquisition of Yutaka Autoparts India.

MAITHAN ALLOYS: Acquires minor stake (~0.02%) in Canara Bank for ₹19.88 crore.

---

📍 COLLABORATION

ZYDUS: Partners with Lupin for co-marketing Semaglutide injection in India.

HGS: Broadband arm partners with UP Government to skill 1 lakh youth.

---

📍 LEADERSHIP CHANGES

CITY UNION BANK: Appoints Ravindra Jadeja as brand ambassador for 2 years.

---

📍 FUNDRAISING

TIL: Announces ₹199 crore rights issue at ₹165 per share.

GNG ELECTRONICS: Secures ₹75 crore enhanced working capital facility from Kotak Mahindra Bank.

---

📍 CLARIFICATION

OLA ELECTRIC: Clarifies no confirmed ₹2,000 crore fundraising for battery arm.

---

📍 LAUNCHES

TCS: Launches NVIDIA-powered ‘Rapid Outcome AI’ platform for enterprise automation.

APIS INDIA: Launches new ‘Masala Dates’ product range.

LT FOODS: Launches premium DAAWAT Saffron Basmati (limited edition).

---

📍 INCORPORATE / CORPORATE STRUCTURE

SHREE CEMENT: Approves incorporation of wholly owned subsidiary in Mauritius.

JITF INFRA: Incorporates step-down subsidiary for smart water metering segment.

---

📍 TAX ORDER

—

---

📍 ADDITIONS

MIDHANI: Receives NADCAP accreditation for heat treatment facility.

---

📍 OTHERS

AUROBINDO PHARMA: Eugia unit gets USFDA OAI status for Bhiwadi facility.

ISGEC HEAVY ENGINEERING: Issues CAD 2 million SBLC for Canada subsidiary.

TEMBO GLOBAL: Subsidiary signs NDA with PSU for defence collaboration.

HINDALCO: Faces near-term pressure due to Novelis plant disruption and rising costs.

CAMLIN FINE SCIENCES: Updates Vinpai tender offer timeline; listing to continue.

GOLDIAM INTERNATIONAL: Expands retail footprint with 2 new stores in Pune.

MARUTI SUZUKI: Focuses on SUVs, EV push and first-time buyers to drive growth.

REFEX INDUSTRIES: Gets exchange nod for amalgamation scheme.

ATHER ENERGY: Expands service network to 500 centres across India.

WEWORK INDIA: Expands Bengaluru capacity with 95,000+ sq ft lease.

---

**🇮🇳 INDIA SEMICONDUCTOR SECTOR – QUICK INVESTMENT SNAPSHOT**

**🧭 OVERALL VIEW**
• Early-stage capex-led upcycle
• Strong tailwinds from AI + electronics + policy
• Still import dependent + execution risk
• Strategy = selective play, not broad betting

**📊 DEMAND DRIVERS**
**Short Term (2–3 yrs)**
• Smartphones + electronics manufacturing boom
• 5G rollout + data centre expansion (AI infra push)
• China+1 shift (Apple, global OEMs)

**Long Term (5–10 yrs)**
• AI devices → higher chip content per device
• EVs + auto electronics + defence demand
• Cloud + AI ecosystem expansion

**🏭 SUPPLY SIDE POSITIONING**
• Strong in design (20% global talent)
• Emerging in ATMP/OSAT (Micron, Tata, CG Power JV)
• Weak in fabs & materials (import heavy)
• Focus = mature nodes (28–65nm), not advanced nodes

**🏗 KEY SEGMENT OPPORTUNITIES**
**Fabs / OSAT (Manufacturing)**
• High capex + high operating leverage
• Beneficiaries: Tata Electronics, Micron, CG Power JV
• Risk: execution + utilisation

**Fabless / IP (Best ROI Segment)**
• Asset-light, high margin
• Focus areas: edge-AI, EV chips, RF, analog
• Strong startup + design ecosystem

**Materials / Equipment (Import Substitution)**
• Huge opportunity (90% import dependency)
• Early stage → long runway
• Policy support via ECMS

**💰 MARGIN DRIVERS**
• Govt subsidy (up to 50% capex support)
• Operating leverage (utilisation ramp-up)
• Product mix (IP > manufacturing margins)
• Raw material import dependency (key risk)

**⚡️ KEY STRUCTURAL TRENDS**
• Shift to compute-driven AI economy
• Supply chain becoming security-driven (not cost-driven)
• India evolving → design → packaging → partial manufacturing
• Policy shift → ecosystem + IP creation focus

**🏛 POLICY SUPPORT**
• ₹76,000 Cr ISM + ₹40,000 Cr ECMS
• 50% subsidy for fabs
• Design incentives (DLI: 4–6% sales support)
• Tax benefits + talent push (85,000 VLSI engineers/year target)

**🌍 GLOBAL CONTEXT**
• Taiwan dominates advanced chips
• US leads design/IP
• China controls materials
• India = design + backend hub emerging

**📈 GROWTH OUTLOOK (2–3 YEARS)**
• Market growth: ~15–18% CAGR
• Early gains from ATMP/OSAT ramp-up
• Fabless design companies gaining traction
• Capex pipeline strong ($15B+ committed)

**⚠️ KEY RISKS**
• Execution delays (infra, fabs)
• High import dependency
• Low R&D spend (~0.6% GDP)
• Talent shortage / brain drain
• Future overcapacity risk

**🎯 INVESTMENT STRATEGY (MOST IMPORTANT)**
**Best Bet (High Conviction)**
• Fabless / IP companies (high margin, scalable)

**Second Layer**
• OSAT / ATMP players (operating leverage play)

**Early Stage Bets**
• Materials & equipment (import substitution theme)

**Avoid / Be Cautious**
• Low value assembly players
• Overhyped fab stories (long gestation, low ROCE risk)

**🧠 ONE LINE TAKE**
• India semiconductor = Design + Packaging story now, Manufacturing story later 🚀

**OIL SHOCK: NIFTY 15-20% RISK**  

**Thesis**  
- $25-30/bbl spike hits GDP 1-2pp, EPS 12%→5-7%, P/E 20x→17-18x.  
- Cyclical; digital/manuf/energy drives rebound.  

**Drivers**  
- GDP 6.5-6.7% to #3 by FY31.  
- Datacenters #2 APAC; EVs/H2/nuclear cut oil use.  
- Exports/domestic buffer.  

**Sectors**  
- **Severe neg**: OMCs (under-recoveries), aviation (ATF).  
- **Neg**: Financials (credit 7-8%), auto/chemicals/paints.  
- **Mild neg**: FMCG.  
- **Pos**: Upstream O&G, pharma, defence, digital infra.  

**Commentary**  
- $10/bbl = 0.2pp GDP drag; RBI delay 2-4Q.  

**Financials**  
- Crude bill $130-140 Bn; Nifty stress P/E 17-18x.  

**Valuations**  
- Normal 20-22x; post-bottom 90%+ gains.  

**Risks**  
- Crude persistence, rupee inflation, fiscal, China/Russia.  

**Triggers**  
- **Near**: Oil stabilise, rupee/RBI/yields.  
- **Med**: Credit revival, structural wins.  

**Bull/Bear**  
- **Bear**: 15-20% drop.  
- **Bull**: Quick peak →90% rally.  

**Key Takeaway:** Buy upstream/digital on weakness; await oil peak.

**2. Growth Drivers**  
- Nifty EPS CAGR: 8.2% FY26E, 16.2% FY27E, 14.8% FY28E (banks/oil/IT/metals).  
- GDP 7%/7.6%/6.8%; upstream/refiners gain short-term.  

**3. Segment Outlook**  
- **Financials**: Tier-2/3 banks/NBFCs cheap P/B.  
- **Consumption**: FMCG/QSR/retail/paints rich fwd P/E – reduce.  
- **Oil&Gas**: Upstream/refiners up; OMCs down.  
- **PSUs/Capgoods**: HAL/BHEL/IRFC/NHPC overvalued vs earnings/NW.  

**4. Broker View**  
- Base: Weeks conflict, months tension; SoH normalizes soon.  
- 1-2Q blips overpriced at 10-15% cuts.  

**5. Financials**  
- Nifty EPS: FY26E ₹1,072, FY27E ₹1,246, FY28E ₹1,432.  
- $10/bbl crude = $20 Bn CAD (0.5% GDP).  

**6. Valuations**  
- Nifty 19x FY27E, 16.5x FY28E.  
- BJFIN FV ₹2,500 (+41%); Eternal ₹375 (+70%).  

**7. Risks**  
- Prolonged SoH disruption/high crude/CAD strain.  
- Elections limit OMC pricing; faster derating.  

**8. Triggers**  
- **Near**: Conflict evolution, govt fuel stance, corp gas commentary.  
- **Medium**: Rates/disruption visibility, earnings delivery.  

**9. Bull/Bear**  
- **Bull**: Quick resolve, buy dips in quality (mid-teens Nifty growth).  
- **Bear**: Prolonged energy shock, broad derating lags earnings.  

**Key Takeaway:** Churn now: Financials in, consumption/PSUs out.

Patent Timeline  
- Semaglutide (Ozempic/Wegovy) patent expires in India on 20 March 2026.

- 5 majors gearing up: Mankind, Ajanta, Sun Pharma, Dr. Reddy's, Lupin.

- Pharma + diagnostics (PharmEasy, Tata 1mg) building integrated solutions.  
- Bundles: drug + diagnostics + follow-up.

- Aggressive ads banned to curb misuse.  
- Tirzepatide (Mounjaro/Zepbound) still patented.  

Key Takeaway: Patent expiry unlocks massive structural tailwind for Indian pharma, hospitals, and digital health.

Sahana System: Positioned for Growth in Anti-Drone Defense Amid Global Tensions

Sahana System Limited is strategically expanding into the anti-drone (Counter-UAS) segment, aligning with rising global demand for advanced defense technologies.

With modern warfare increasingly driven by drone-based threats, governments are accelerating investments in detection, jamming and electronic warfare systems.

Sahana is capitalizing on this shift through RF and radar-based detection, AI-powered threat intelligence and integrated counter-drone solutions.

The company’s recent ₹24.69 crore order from Central Electronics Limited highlights strong execution and entry into defense electronics.

Partnership with Bharat Electronics Limited strengthens its position within India’s defense ecosystem.

Increasing demand from military, border security and critical infrastructure protection continues to support growth.

As geopolitical tensions persist, anti-drone technology is emerging as a critical defense priority.

reports.text:
*Kotak Inst Eqt on Aster DM*
Reinstate ADD, TP Rs 725
Expect Aster DM, after its imminent merger with Quality Care (QCIL), to report robust 19%/22% pro forma sales/EBITDA CAGRs over FY2026-28E.
Besides synergies, there are multiple other levers such as higher oncology mix, improved payor mix, lower ALOS for QCIL and MVT to drive margin expansion.
Backed by a strong balance sheet and a calibrated expansion of 4,342 beds until FY2030E, out of which 60% are brownfield, believe the Aster DM-QCIL combo has a multi-year profitable growth runway ahead.

*Kotak Inst Eqt on Food Delivery cos*
shortage of LPG in the country due to the war in West Asia and the redistribution of the existing supplies to priority sectors is driving a shortage of commercial LPG supply to restaurants
This could impact orders available for food delivery and possibly higher menu prices.
A complete month-long disruption in food delivery could adversely impact Swiggy’s FY2026E FD EBITDA by 21% and Zomato’s FY2026E FD EBITDA by 16%.
While impact will be temporary, Swiggy’s stock could witness a bigger impact, given larger contribution of FD to its valuation.

*Kotak Inst Eqt on OMCs*
Are raising oil price assumptions to US$85/bbl for FY2027, US$75/bbl for FY2028
Ongoing West Asia crisis & Strait of Hormuz (SoH) disruption skew risks to upside in FY2027
With no retail pricing freedom, OMCs will have to absorb higher crude and freight/insurance costs
Negative public sentiment amid LPG shortages makes large petrol/diesel price hikes very difficult
OMCs have benefited from elevated marketing margins in past few years
However, weak earnings are now set to erode buffer created
Post-crisis, new capex for LPG storage is likely
Reiterate SELL on all OMCs
IOC – TP cut to Rs 100 from Rs 125
BPCL – TP cut to Rs 240 from Rs 300
HPCL – TP cut to Rs235 from Rs335

*Nuvama on Marico*
Buy, TP Rs 900
Management meet Insights
1)VAHO to remain strong (18-quarter high growth of 29% in Q3FY26).
2)Middle East comprises 3–4% of consolidated revenue with no significant impact on overall revenue currently.
3)Copra correction (~35% from peak) has not been passed on yet, but a calibrated price cut is likely soon.
4)Bangladesh’s political landscape is now stable and growth momentum shall sustain (29% CC growth in Q3FY26).
Ongoing Middle East tensions remain a key monitorable, as they may lead to potential raw material and packaging costs inflation

*HSBC on Metals*
Given the disruption in gas supplies and surge in prices, regional steel prices have increased by 5-7% with export prices now near USD500/t
Given INR weakness, implied landed prices are now in Rs 58,000 range vs domestic HRC prices of Rs 56,000
Given demand uncertainty, do not expect steel mills to raise prices for now.
For Indian mills, a sharp decline in coking coal prices (down 11% in last 1 month) is positive, supporting margins in 1HFY27
For aluminium, while LME has been flattish this week, premiums have surged with MJP premiums above USD200/t given disruption in aluminium shipments from Middle East

*HSBC on Tata Motors PV*
Hold, TP cut to Rs 340 from Rs 400
Middle East exposure and rising raw material costs have further added to the woes of JLR
India business benefits from strong Sierra and incremental Harrier petrol demand, though RM inflation is a risk to margins
JLR’s recovery in the near term appears challenging

*Nomura on Autos*
Parliamentary panel recommends EV incentives
Ather, SONACOMS, MM, TMPV, & TVS could be key beneficiaries
Further government support should accelerate EV adoption
A potential relaxation in PLI eligibility norms could be a significant positive for Ather Energy
faster EV penetration will be a key positive for SONACOMs followed by Uno Minda

*MOSL on Hindalco*
Buy, TP Rs 1110
Management meet takeaways
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
Management expects domestic demand (across Asia) to remain robust & outpace modest growth expectation of 2-4% CAGR globally, broadly driven by renewable & electrification, infra spending, packaging and auto/EV adoption
Impact of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East is largely limited to rising energy (coal) costs
Management noted that 75% of energy is fulfilled via coal linkages & rest via e-auction; therefore, rise in coal e-auction prices can increase its energy costs
HNDL’s cost mix stands as 40% coal, 35% alumina and 25% others (incl. caustic soda and petcoke/CP coke, etc.)
As a mitigation strategy, HNDL targets to be 100% captive by FY33 via three captive mines (Chakla – 1HFY27, Bandha - FY27 and Meenakshi - FY29), resulting in ~USD200/t in direct cost savings
Management plans to expand value-added product offerings (both copper and aluminum), with an aim of cushioning the margins and achieving higher downstream EBITDA in India over the medium term
HNDL highlighted that Aditya FRP and battery enclosure facility is currently ramping up, while it is expecting the commissioning of the IGT, battery foil and AC fin.

*Jefferies on JSW Infra*
Buy, TP Rs 360
JSW Infra management in recent interactions reiterated its target to double EBITDA over FY26E-28E, led by capacity additions which are on track.
Logistics (5% of FY26E EBITDA) growth plans are ahead of schedule.
Geopolitical tensions are a near-term headwind
Raise FY28E EBITDA by 10%, to reflect the optimism on project progress and estimate 29% FY26E-30E EBITDA CAGR, led by 19% volume CAGR, where group expansion plans lend utilisation visibility.

*Jefferies on Power Demand*
Feb 2026 power demand rose just 1% YoY on a base of 2% YoY growth in Feb 2025.
Apr 2025-Feb 2026 demand is up only 1% YoY largely driven by above average monsoons.
Our 2% FY26E power demand growth assumption needs 14% YoY rise in March 2026.
Generation is up just 2% YoY in the first 12 days of March but with rising temperatures should recover ahead.
JSW Energy & NTPC remain top picks

*Jefferies on Finolex Cables*
Buy, TP Rs 1025
Management meet takeaways
FNXC took 5 price hikes, total +12% in Dec25-Q, & 2 hikes in Jan-Feb26 (+5%) to pass-on copper surge

India's BESS Boom: Capacity Surge, Execution Risk  

Market Momentum:  
- Sharp rise in standalone BESS tendering over past year.  
- Solar + BESS now cheapest dispatchable power, extending beyond solar hours.  
- BESS shifting from optional to essential in power stack.  

Capacity Pipeline:  
- ~31 GWh (~15 GW) standalone BESS by FY28 (awarded + ongoing tenders).  
- Phasing: FY26: ~2 GWh; FY27: ~10 GWh; FY28: ~19 GWh.  
- Assumes ~1 year from award to commissioning.  

Key Players:  
- State utilities driving bulk of near-term awards.  
- NTPC strategic participant.  
- Private/non-utility players winning majority capacity.  

Tariff Trends:  
- Early tenders: ₹1.8L+ per MWh/month.  
- Recent wins: ₹70k-₹90k per MWh/month.  
- Driven by falling battery costs, intense competition, aggressive entry bids.  

Structural Shift:  
- Optimizes transmission, balances renewables.  
- Reduces need for pumped storage, incremental thermal capacity.  
- Focuses on system efficiency, not capacity inflation.  

Execution Risks:  
- Thin margins from aggressive tariffs.  
- Battery sourcing volatility, financing stress.  
- Untested EPC/O&M at scale, potential commissioning delays.  

Investor Red Flags:  
- Low entry barriers attracting underprepared players.  
- Non-utilities dominating wins, limited operating history.  
- Expect attrition, consolidation, slippages.  

Key Takeaway:  
BESS capacity is locked in, but execution discipline separates survivors from casualties.

- Buyer: STEL Holdings Ltd  
- Target: Saregama India Ltd  
- Shares Acquired: 3,50,000 equity shares (Face Value: Re 1)

- Entity Type: Unregistered Core Investment Company, part of promoter group  
- Transaction Nature: Ordinary course of business, not related-party  
- Purpose: Investment in promoter group portfolio

reports.text:
MOSL on Hindalco

Buy, TP Rs 1110

Management meet takeaways,

Management expects domestic demand (across Asia) to remain robust & outpace modest growth expectation of 2-4% CAGR globally, broadly driven by renewable & electrification, infra spending, packaging and auto/EV adoption

Impact of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East is largely limited to rising energy (coal) costs

Management noted that 75% of energy is fulfilled via coal linkages & rest via e-auction; therefore, rise in coal e-auction prices can increase its energy costs

HNDL’s cost mix stands as 40% coal, 35% alumina and 25% others (incl. caustic soda and petcoke/CP coke, etc.)

As a mitigation strategy, HNDL targets to be 100% captive by FY33 via three captive mines (Chakla – 1HFY27, Bandha - FY27 and Meenakshi - FY29), resulting in ~USD200/t in direct cost savings

Management plans to expand value-added product offerings (both copper and aluminum), with an aim of cushioning the margins and achieving higher downstream EBITDA in India over the medium term

HNDL highlighted that Aditya FRP and battery enclosure facility is currently ramping up, while it is expecting the commissioning of the IGT, battery foil and AC fin.

reports.text:
Mankind Pharma Plans Early Semaglutide Launch in India

Mankind Pharma aims to be among the first companies to launch semaglutide in India after the drug’s patent expires on March 20.

India will be the primary launch market, while international markets will be explored later.

The company plans to launch the drug independently, with no co-marketing or partnership arrangements.

Semaglutide is widely used for diabetes and weight management, and demand is rising amid growing obesity and metabolic health concerns.

Mankind plans to price the drug carefully to balance affordability and long-term sustainability, continuing its strategy of introducing affordable medicines.

The company expects strong market expansion, as the patent expiry could allow around 50 generic brands to enter the Indian market.

Beyond diabetes, semaglutide has potential applications across cardiology, orthopaedics, and gynaecology, expanding its therapeutic scope.

Indian Metals & Ferro Alloys Ltd — Scaling Ferrochrome & Mining Expansion

MCap: ₹6,814.9 Cr 
Industry: Mining & Minerals

Production Targets
- FY27 ferrochrome target: 4,00,000 tonnes
- FY28 ferrochrome target: 4,75,000–5,00,000 tonnes
- Ore raising scaling to 1 million tonnes by FY27

Capacity Expansion — KNR
- KNR1 furnace 1: commissioned June 2026
- KNR1 furnace 2: commissioned September 2026
- Tata Kalinganagar (KNR2) acquisition closing Q4 FY26
- KNR2 volumes contributing from Q1 FY27

Cost Advantages
- EBITDA cost to fall ₹1,500–2,000/tonne on logistics gains
- Fully captive ore supply — zero external ore purchases
- 110 MW hybrid renewable PPAs signed at ₹3.75–3.84/unit from June 2026

Sales Mix Shift
- Current: 90% export / 10% domestic
- Target: 60% export / 40% domestic over 2 years

Mining Upgrade
- Sukinda mine: 300 kt open-cast → 600 kt underground
- Underground mining capex: ₹1,000 Cr over 4–5 years

New Business — Ethanol
- 120 KLD ethanol plant commissioning March 2026
- Capex: ₹150 Cr | Contribution from FY27

Capital Allocation
- FY26 total capex: ₹800–900 Cr — funded via internal accruals
- Peak debt ceiling: ₹470 Cr
- Target D/E: ≤0.3x

Realization Outlook
- Domestic: ₹1,18,000–1,20,000/tonne
- Export: $0.96–0.97/lb
- South African supply constraints + chrome ore inflation = supportive pricing

Key Takeaway
- Volume nearly 2x by FY28 vs current levels
- Cost reduction + captive ore = strong margin expansion ahead
- Clean balance sheet with disciplined capex funding
- Renewable energy + domestic mix shift = ESG & margin tailwind

The Shrinking MOAT — A Risk Investors Are Underpricing

The Core Observation
- Sanjoy Bhattacharya: *"Period of competitive advantage has shortened dramatically worldwide"*
- MOATs that once lasted decades are now lasting years
- Rate of disruption is accelerating faster than ever

The Valuation Trap
- Assigning high terminal value to MOATs = hidden risk
- Markets price in perpetual competitive advantage
- When MOAT breaks → sudden & sharp de-rating event
- Multiples built on MOATs can become irrational until they collapse

Why MOATs Are Breaking Faster
- Democratisation of capital — funding is more accessible
- Technology — levels the playing field rapidly
- AI — disrupts conventional business models at scale
- Lower barriers — new entrants can replicate faster than before

30 Years Ago vs Today
- Earlier: MOATs were extremely difficult to break
- Today: Competitive advantages are far more vulnerable
- Conventional business models now face real terminal value risk

The Nuanced View
- Some MOATs do deserve premium multiples
- But assuming perpetuity is where the danger lies
- Investors must ask: *"Is this MOAT still intact — or just priced as if it is?"*

Key Takeaway
- Price in MOAT durability — not just MOAT existence
- A broken MOAT + high multiple = significant wealth destruction
- In today's world, re-examine every terminal value assumption

West Asia Tensions Weigh on India’s Gems & Jewellery Exports  

Export Pressure Mounts  
- India’s gems and jewellery exports have declined ~20%, impacted by geopolitical instability in West Asia, per GJEPC.  
- If disruptions persist, exports worth ~$1.2 billion could be hit in the next three months.  

Regional Dependence  
- About 20% of India’s jewellery exports are directed to West Asia, exposing the sector to regional volatility.  
- The UAE and GCC together account for ~$8.3 billion in exports, with Dubai serving as a major hub for polished diamond trading.  

Trade Linkages at Risk  
- Gold imports to the UAE (~200 tonnes annually) could face hurdles, given the India–UAE CEPA dependencies in the supply chain.  

Broader Context  
- From Apr 2025–Feb 2026, exports were flat at $25.93 billion, nearly unchanged from $25.92 billion YoY, reflecting a sluggish global demand cycle.  
- The industry is also navigating global trade uncertainties and potential US tariff pressures, further straining export momentum.  

Key Takeaway:  
Persistent West Asia tensions and global trade headwinds could delay recovery for India’s jewellery export sector, underscoring the need for market diversification and supply-chain resilience.

**Why SIPs Thrive in Crashes: 2008 Proof**  

**Peak vs. Dip Reality**  
- **Jan 2008 Peak (NAV ₹16.84)**: ₹10K SIP → **79K** (XIRR 12.8%), **593 units**.  

**Power of Falling NAVs**  
- NAV crashes to **₹12, 9, 7, 6, 5** → SIP buys **~1,000 units** (more shares at bargain prices).  

**Dip Installments Win Big**  
- **2009 Bottom SIP**: ₹10K → **₹2.8L** (₹2.7L gain vs. ₹69K at peak).  
- **Similar holding periods**, but **exponential unit accumulation** drives superior returns.  

**Strategy Edge**  
- **Continue SIPs**: Rupee cost averaging maximizes long-term compounding.  
- **Legends top up**; **ultra-legends lump sum** on deep dips.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Falling markets = **SIP superpower**—buy more units cheap, ride the rebound for outsized gains like 2008’s 28x bottom returns. Stay invested.

- Peak-cycle earnings over: build in lower, volatile marketing margins for OMCs.  
- Upside now valuation/technical, not fundamentals at elevated crude.  
- ONGC acts as policy-capped upstream utility, not pure crude play.

**JEFFERIES ON BANKS: LIMITED BUYBACKS, DIVIDEND POTENTIAL AHEAD**

**Global Buyback Boom**  
- Global banks doubled buybacks to **>$260B in 2025**, driven by higher ROE, excess capital, and slow growth.

**Indian Banks' Parallel Setup**  
- Indian banks show **rising ROE** and **slowing growth** (still above global peers).  
- Jefferies sees **limited buyback scope** despite similar tailwinds.

**Dividend Cap Hike Unlocks Value**  
- Recent **hike in dividend payout cap** enables select banks to lift shareholder returns.

**High Capital, High Payout Candidates**  
- **HDFC Bank, Union Bank, Bank of India** lead with strong capital and payout flexibility.

**Capital-Rich but Cautious Players**  
- **Kotak Bank, CSB Bank** have high capitalization but have kept payouts low.

**Key Takeaway:**  
**Prioritize dividend upside in HDFC/PSUs over buybacks — ROE strength favors payout hikes in a capped repurchase environment.**

BROKERAGE RADAR

Evaluating Buyback Optionality Among Indian Banks

In 2025, buybacks by global banks doubled to >$260 billion aided by higher ROE, capital and slow growth

Indian banks' ROE has risen & growth has slowed, but is still above peers

So we see limited scope for buybacks, but with hike in cap on dividend, some can raise payout

HDFC, Union Bank, and Bank of India have higher capital and payout

Kotak Bank and CSB have higher capitalisation, but have kept payouts low
Iran 🇮🇷 has ~10× the population and far larger geography, yet Israel’s 🇮🇱 economy is bigger and its income per person is ~10× higher.

**India’s Credit Paradox – NBFCs & MSME Credit Gap**

• **NBFCs share:** ~21% of total credit  
• **Banks share:** Majority of remaining credit

• **Core issue:** Not a capital shortage, but a **credit translation problem**

• **Credit gap:** MSMEs, microfinance & small-ticket borrowers face **₹25–30 trillion deficit**

• **Economic role:** These segments contribute **~30–40% of India’s GDP**

• **Employment:** Employ **majority of India’s non-farm workforce**

reports.text:
**INDIA EV AUTO: LEGACY OEMs DOMINATE UPTREND**

**Big Picture**  
EV upcycle intact across E-2W/3W/PV, but **legacy OEMs consolidating** (~80% share) as startups falter on service/capital/execution. FY26 volumes: E-2W >1.3 Mn (+15%), E-PV ~200k (+84%), E-3W +16% YTD.

**E-2W Leadership**  
TVS **28%**, Bajaj **23%**, Ather **18%**, Hero **11%** = **80% share**.  
TVS iQube peaks ~35k/mo; Bajaj Chetak C2501 <₹1L; Ather Rizta family pivot; Hero VIDA BaaS ₹45k + ₹0.96/km.  
**Ola collapse**: ~53k → ~4k/mo on service woes.

**E-PV Concentration**  
Tata **40%**, JSW MG **25-30%**, Mahindra **15%** = **85%**.  
Tata full-stack (Tiago to Harrier.ev, BaaS Punch); Mahindra BE6/XEV 9e scaling to 7-8k pm FY27; JSW Windsor >75%, MG 520 PHEV Oct'26.  
Maruti e-Vitara entry; Hyundai/BYD ~3-4%.

**E-3W Acceleration**  
Fastest growth; fleet-driven (passenger/e-comm).  
Bajaj overtakes Mahindra; TVS +1400% YTD low-base. Subsidy end Dec'25 but momentum holds.

**Company Spotlights**  
**TVS**: iQube + Orbiter; ~33% EV growth vs 15% industry.  
**Bajaj**: Chetak/GoGo; 2W/3W swapping.  
**Mahindra**: Treo/e-Alfa ~12% 3W EV share FY26; XEV 9S deliveries.  
**Tata Motors**: Punch/Harrier/Curvv ramps.

**Themes**  
Service networks/reliability win; TCO > subsidies; GST ICE cuts narrow gap but EVs hold on running costs (~₹0.2/km vs ₹2 petrol). BaaS widens access.

**Key Takeaway**  
Legacy scale (TVS/Bajaj/Tata/Mahindra) trumps startups—position for consolidation phase.

Asia’s Hormuz Exposure Scorecard – Nomura

• Energy Security Risk: Asia is at the epicenter of potential energy supply shocks if disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz continue.

• Stagflation Risk: Prolonged disruption (>1 month) could trigger stagflation — higher inflation with slower growth.

• Most Exposed Economies:
  – Thailand
  – South Korea
  – India

• Key Reason: High dependence on Middle East oil and gas imports passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

Implication:  
Sustained disruption could lead to higher oil prices, rising inflation and slower economic growth across Asia.

Ramp in Mumbai/Pune/Maharashtra, south (Hyderabad/Bangalore/Kolkata; Bangalore land acquired).

**QSR Outlook**  
High single-digits FY27; frozen 20% → 30% of QSR revenue.  
Biscuits/QSR weaker (high single-digits); bakery/exports stronger.

**Risks**  
Supply chain disruptions; plant consolidation/capex execution.

**Key Takeaway**  
Bakery/export momentum supports re-rating as valuations derate with earnings growth.

**Business Strength**

• Gold loans form **~89% of total AUM**.
• Strong branch network drives customer growth.

**Strong Growth Momentum**

**Robust Loan Growth**
• **Q3FY26 AUM: ₹1,64,720 Cr (↑ 48% YoY)**.
• **Gold loan AUM: ₹1,47,552 Cr (↑ 51% YoY)**.
• **FY26 gold loan growth guidance: ~45%**.

**Profitability**

**Sharp Earnings Growth**
• **Q3FY26 PAT: ₹2,656 Cr**.
• **9MFY26 PAT: ₹7,048 Cr (↑ 91% YoY)**.
• **FY26E PAT expected: ₹9,687 Cr**.

**High Return Ratios**
• **FY26E ROE: ~29.6%**.

**Asset Quality & Risk**

**Improving Asset Quality**
• **Stage III gold loans: 1.58%** vs **4.22% last year**.

**Strong Collateral Safety**
• **Average LTV: 57%**.
• **~43% margin of safety** against gold price volatility.

**Margins**

**High Yield Business**

**Valuation**

**Target Price**

• **Target: ₹3,478**.
• **Upside: ~10%**.

**Broker View**
• Rating upgraded to **HOLD**.
• Strong growth but **valuations already premium**.

**CITI: MIDDLE EAST RISKS TEST BANKS**

**Macro Vulnerabilities**  
Geopolitical escalation adds financial risks via:
- Elevated crude prices  
- Trade flow disruptions  
- Currency/liquidity volatility  
- Widening CAD  

**Sector Strength**  
Banks enter from **decade-low GNPAs + robust CET-1**.

**Prolonged Conflict Risks**  
Pressures cascade to asset quality, liquidity, market risk, credit growth.

**Defensive Hierarchy**  
**Private banks > PSUs > NBFCs**.

**Preferred Picks**  
**HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, RBL Bank**.

**Key Takeaway:**  
Strong fundamentals cushion blow—stick to top-tier private banks for relative safety.

**UBS: FOOD DELIVERY VOLUMES +20% YoY**

**February Receipts Data**  
Industry volumes surge **20-21% YoY**.

**Three Key Trends**  
- **FD growth** sustains >20% YoY; Mar-Apr risk from cooking gas shortages  
- **Market shares stable** in Q4 first two months  
- **Blinkit** pulls ahead in quick commerce reach vs Instamart/Zepto  

**Ratings & Targets**  
- **Eternal**: BUY, TP ₹375  
- **Swiggy**: BUY, TP ₹510  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Robust 20%+ growth intact—Blinkit execution favors Eternal/Swiggy despite gas risks.

**UBS: EUREKA FORBES BUY @ ₹640**

**Initiation Drivers**  
**Initiate BUY** underpinned by four catalysts:

**1. Category Inflection**  
Underpenetrated **water purifiers (45% revenue)** hit takeoff phase.

**2. Share Gains**  
Capex/R&D/product innovation drives **~150 bps market share gain FY25-28**.

**3. Margin Expansion**  
Higher-margin service (AMC) + filters lift margins **250 bps FY25-28**.

**4. Category Tailwinds**  
Vacuum cleaners/air purifiers at **18% CAGR FY26-30** on low penetration.

**Projections**  
Revenue/EBITDA CAGR: **13%****/22****% FY25-28**.

**Key Takeaway:**  
Four-pronged thesis positions EFL for re-rating from underpenetrated categories + margin levers.
